clauseList.text: The Supplier may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon five (5) days written notice.
clauseList.simplifiedText: The supplier can end the agreement for any reason with only five days notice.
clauseList.explanation: This gives the supplier broad exit rights and leaves the customer exposed operationally.
clauseList.counterClauseSuggestion: Either party may terminate for convenience upon thirty (30) days written notice after completing all committed deliverables due in that period.

clauseList.text: Invoices are due within fifteen (15) days of receipt.
clauseList.simplifiedText: Invoices must be paid within 15 days.
clauseList.explanation: The payment term is short and may pressure cash flow.
clauseList.counterClauseSuggestion: Invoices are due within thirty (30) days of receipt unless otherwise agreed in writing.

clauseList.text: Each party will comply with all applicable privacy laws.
clauseList.simplifiedText: Both sides must follow privacy laws.
clauseList.explanation: This is balanced baseline compliance language.

aiOutput.summary: The agreement is workable but contains unilateral termination and aggressive payment timing.
recommendations: Extend convenience termination notice to 30 days.

lawyerOutput.reviewNotes: Priority should be placed on termination rights and limitation of liability review.
